summary: "Use the QoS file for all QoS, with topic-category-specific profiles"
description: """Specifically [~<USER> would like to try making events use TRANSIENT_LOCAL durability and telemetry use BEST_EFFORT reliability. But more generally we would like to be able to make such changes by modifying the QoS file, instead of changing code in ts_sal and ts_salobj. So [~<USER> and I have agreed to put all topic QoS in the QoS file with separate profiles: AckcmdProfile, CommandProfile, EventProfile, and Telemetry Profile. The new QoS file is in ts_idl: qos/QoS.xml.    Note that ts_salobj still sets read queue depth programmatically, though the default value of 100 matches the read and write depth in the current QoS file. This might be useful for making a longer read queue for certain systems, such as SAL/kafka producers. But if we don't need it, I can simplify the code by using the value in the QoS file. One problem with setting this programmatically is that there is no simple way for Python to determine what is in the QoS from the file. So if the depth is changed in the QoS file the default has to be changed manually in ts_salobj (and the problem is further complicated if we decide to have different depths for different topic categories)."""

summary: "Correct TIMESYS header keyword in CORR and CALEXP"
description: """The TIMESYS header keyword is modified from UTC in the raw files to TAI in the corr and calexp files, but the time values are unchanged. This should be fixed so either the TIMESYS is not modified or so that the values are properly converted."""
comments: "The value for 'TIMESYS' has been hardcoded to 'TAI' in `afw/src/image/VisitInfo.cc` (line 290). This could be changed to use the TIMESYS value from the raw files, but currently VisitInfo.cc will throw an exception (line 353) if the TIMESYS is anything other than TAI." [SEP] "VisitInfo is set to TAI for decam and subaru isn't it? In python/lsst/obs/base/makeRawVisitInfoViaObsInfo.py so downstream files are using TAI even if raw is using UTC.    What instrument is showing this behavior?" [SEP] "Oh, sorry. The problem is that VisitInfo writes out a DATE-AVG in TAI but does not delete the DATE-OBS or DATE-END headers if they exist. This means that you get one TAI time and two UTC times (but for LSSTCam the DATE-OBS will be TAI). TIMESYS is a fairly big sledgehammer in the FITS standard and easy to mess up. It's probable that the only approach here is for the VisitInfo writer to check for the presence of TIMESYS and convert to whatever timescale is already there."

At this point it seems likely that Formatter is only used for files and that we can clean up some wrinkles in the structure of the classes by making that explicit. If a new datastore comes along that needs something other than files and needs a Formatter concept then we can create a whole new unrelated class for it."""

summary: "Handle the flux at the edge of footprints in meas_extensions_scarlet"
description: """The footprints passed to scarlet appear to be smaller than the flux that scarlet wants to model, at least in the observed images, which means that it is fairly common to have flux outside of the footprint that scarlet wants/needs for its model. Because scarlet models are in a frame with a narrower PSF than the observations, the scarlet models fit inside of the footprints but have no constraints on the portion of the flux outside the footprint, which are often smaller than the radius of the PSF from faint sources. As a result as much as 15-20% of the flux in a faint source is not accounted for (see attached image for an example).    Based on conversations with Peter, Robert, and Jim, the best solutions appears to be to create an algorithm to estimate the likelihood that all of the flux for a source on the edge of a scarlet model is statistically consistent with zero, and to flag any sources that appear to have significant flux outside of the footprint. This could also explain why scarlet models have a consistent bias below the true value for fake sources at the 1% level.    In the short term the solution (which is to be resolved by this ticket) is to create and implement the flag for sources that underestimate their flux. A longer term solution is saved for a later ticket."""

summary: "Persist individual bright stars' warping Transforms and preserve stamp origin"
description: """This ticket will make two minor changes to ProcessBrightStarsTask:         DM-23786 relies on saved bright star """"stamps"""", that have already been preprocessed (DM-25304), to build an extended PSF model.    The preprocessing done includes a warping of each stamp to the same common grid. In order to subtract a bright star, the invert transform needs to be applied to the model first.    In this ticket, we will make sure the actual `lsst.afw.geom.TransformPoint2ToPoint2` built during preprocessing (see [here|https://github.com/lsst/pipe_tasks/blob/tickets/DM-25304/python/lsst/pipe/tasks/processBrightStars.py#L287]) is saved along with the stamp itself.         In addition, instead of setting the origin of each stamp to an arbitrary value at this stage, change processBrightStarsTask to preserve the initial stamp XY0. While the origin will still need to be set to the same value for stacking (DM-25305), it makes subtraction (DM-23786) a lot easier if the initial XY0 is preserved; there really is no need to delete that information at the preprocessing stage.    Similarly, applying afwMath.rotateImageBy90 resets XY0 to (0,0). Instead of applying it during preprocessing (as is done now), keep the stamps in their initial orientation, and save (a single int containing) the number of necessary rotations for cross-detector stacking in header."""

summary: "Try ignoring extra track commands in ts_rotator"
description: """Rotator can get behind. Try ignoring extra commands.    If this works and is wanted then ts_salobj probably wants an update to handle it more gracefully (so ackcmds come for the commands actually executed, even though the pointing components are ignoring those acks). But I sincerely hope we will not need to do this. The existing queue is sufficient for getting a bit behind and catching back up, and I don't think we want to be running in a mode where the CSC cannot keep up overall. It would be far better to fix the problem -- which could be some combination of:  * Profile the CSC and try to remove bottlenecks.  * Slow down the tracking rate to 10 Hz. This should be plenty.  * Run on faster hardware."""

summary: "Replace sleep with conditional variable in Controller thread, C++11 solutions for threads etc."
description: """M1M3 SS C++ code is:   * locking shared mutex   * trying to get command   * unlocking shared mutex   * if command is valid, executes it   * waits for 100us    Mouch better solution would be to use contional variable, linked to queue size, which will be trigged as new element is added and execute method would wait for CV trigger. This will remove need for sleep and continuously checking for queue content. As the queue is private in the Controller class, managed by public methods, this shall be easy to provide.    As a side improvement, threading structure of the M1M3 SS main code can be improved using C++11 STL provided threading constructs. I will try to do this as well."""

summary: "Support producing Kafka messages for OpenSplice internal DDS topics"
description: """Support a sal/kafka producer that handles OpenSplice internal DDS topics so we can get this useful diagnostic information into the EFD.    The simplest technique is probably to allow creating a SalInfo for these, so we can make Remotes that listen to them. The trick will be parsing the IDL file, since it has no revision hash codes (nor metadata comments).    We realize that when things start to go wrong we may no longer see these messages. But it's worth a try. In the long run we also want to get the standard log output into a central location, which should be more reliable when DDS has problems. We we can probably learn a lot from the data that does get into the EFD."""

summary: "Improve ScriptQueue handling of scripts that fail before running."
description: """Currently when a script fails to configure or some other failure while loading (before running), the scrip queue provides little feedback on what happened to it. The script simply disappears from the queue going from the {{salIndices}} to nowhere.     I think it would be better if those scripts also land in the {{pastSalIndices}} with some information on which stage it failed (loading? configuring?). """
comments: "The current feedback is that the ScriptInfo script event outputs processState=config failed.    I am willing to put such scripts on the history as that will clearly make it easier to see what went wrong. I will try to also prevent requeueing such scripts, since they will definitely fail again (unless the script is edited before requeueing, which seems too much of a stretch)." [SEP] "The new code moves *all* scripts to the history, whether they fail or are stopped. Note that when stopping a collection of scripts the order in which they appear in the history is not deterministic. I added support to {{test_script_queue.py}} and {{test_queue_model.py}} to handle this: if {{past_sal_indices}} is a set then order is ignored.    I decided not to try to block requeue of scripts that failed reconfiguration as I think it makes the system overly complicated. We can add that later if necessary.    I also overhauled the documentation to improve it and use the modern layout.    Pull request: https://github.com/lsst-ts/ts_

On task that exists in gen2, but not yet in gen3 is the utility that makes a discrete sky map from a collection of calibrated exposures: {{makeDiscreteSkyMap.py}}.  This utility is useful especially in scenarios where the visits are relatively localized on the sky.    This ticket is to implement a new subcommand using the {{click}} module.  I don't see why this couldn't be called {{make_discrete_sky_map}}.  This will involve a few steps.  1) Wrap the {{MakeDiscreteSkyMapTask}} with the necessary gen3 tooling  2) Pull the bulk of the heavy lifting out of the {{runDataRef}} method into a {{run}} method that both {{runDataRef}} and {{runQuantum}} can delegate to  3) Add a subcommand to {{butler}} that calls that task."""

description: """[~<USER> - Please wait to do this until the ATArchiver and CCArchiver tickets are completed for this.  There are possible changes in dm_csc_base.  Waiting for these tickets will make doing this one easier.         From @<USER>in #ts-software Slack:         please update your CSCs to be compatible with ts_salobj 6. You can easily retain version 5 compatibility at the same time. The version history is here: [https://ts-salobj.lsst.io/v/PR-141/version_history.html]. Basically ts_salobj 6 removes all the things in 5 that were deprecated (most or all of which should be printing deprecation warnings). Some highlights:  • use {{BaseCsc.amain}} instead of {{main}} to start your CSCs (in your bin script).  • The {{initial_simulation_mode}} CSC constructor argument is no longer accepted. Use {{simulation_mode}} instead.  • The {{fault}} method now requires an error code.  • Do not set the summary_state attribute in CSCs; it is read-only. Most code I have seen that does this is going into fault state; call the {{fault}} method for that.  Also for later, when you no longer need ts_salobj 5 compatibility: version 6 offers simplified support for simulation mode. See the manual (how to write a CSC, simulation section) and recent changes to ts_ATDome to see how it works. Meanwhile you will see a deprecation warning when you start CSCs that support simulation."""

summary: "Add ability to prune erroneous datasetTypes from gen3 registry"
description: """During development of a {{pipelinetask}} it may happen that the developer will erroneously define an incorrect {{datasetType}} (with incorrect {{dimensions}}, or {{storageClass}}, etc.). At this point it is impossible to recover without blowing away the registry or sqlite hacking because any fix to the entry will raise a {{ValueError: Supplied dataset type inconsistent with registry definition}} error.      We should not allow the ability to delete datasets if a dataset type is associated with them that is being deleted. This would imply that the dataset type has been used. Instead a user should first delete the relevant collection/dataset before deleting the dataset type."""
comments: "This should probably be accompanied by a command-line tool (as a {{butler}} subcommand)." [SEP] "I was wondering about the user interface. How brave are we allowing anyone to force delete anything (by mistake using --force on a calexp dataset type for example). As opposed to having pipetask have an option for overriding the dataset type definition (by pruning and re-registering)." [SEP] "The database constraints as defined will guarantee that we get an exception unless there are no datasets of this dataset type at all in the repo, and I was planning to embrace that and leave it to users to clean up datasets first." [SEP] "Ok, so you **don't** want the proposed {{force=True}} option then." [SEP] "I think that's where I stand, though I realize now I had only skimmed the original ticket description and hadn't noticed that part of it until now.    Basically, it's really easy to write a super safe version of this without {{\-\-force}} because the database constraint does all of the work, and it's a pain to write

summary: "Include instrument data ID value when provided on pipetask command-line or Pipeline yaml file"
description: """An instrument name needs to be provided by pipetask (as an explicit argument, or via the Pipeline object) in order to load obs_* package overrides, and generally should also be provided as part of the -d argument that constraints data IDs, and it is confusing to users that providing an instrument in one place does not satisfy the other.    For now, I think we should focus on passing the instrument from the Pipeline or explicit argument to the data ID constraint, because it's impossible in general to extract an instrument from the latter even if it ought to be possible for most exceptions.  I think this is as simple as taking the original -d argument and rewriting it as """"instrument='{instrument}' AND ({original})"""".  If there's already an instrument in the original, it should be a harmless."""
comments: "Andy, can you please review?" All visit methods return tuple of (type, value), non-useful nodes      return None for both type and value.      """"""      def __init__(self):          self.instruments = []        def visitNumericLiteral(self, value, node):          # do not care about numbers          return (None, None)        def visitStringLiteral(self, value, node):          # return type and value          return (""str"", val

summary: "Prohibit dimension query expressions that do not reference required dependencies"
description: """Constraining """"tract"""" without """"skymap"""" or """"visit""""/""""exposure""""/""""detector"""" without """"instrument"""" is an easy mistake for new Gen3 users to make due to history, and we've recently learned that even in repos with just one skymap and/or instrument, this can come with a huge performance penalty.  When validating a QueryWhereExpression, check that all required dimensions are referenced, and raise if they are not.  This will not complete guard against bad constraints (""""tract = 1 OR (skymap = 'mine' AND tract=2))"""" would not be flagged as bad, but it would become much harder to produce a bad expression by accident."""

summary: "Investigate aperture correction measurement error when running ProcessCcd on Decam galactic bulge images"
description: """As reported in DM-25694, 6145 exposures (out of 20,679) failed with fatal errors during single frame processing of DECam galactic bulge data (in i- and g-band). Of these, 166 were RuntimeErrors, e.g.        and      I need to identify the cause, or causes, of this error and suggest a solution. Implementation of the solution is expected to be made on a new ticket."""
comments: "Following the changes made on DM-26586 and DM-26584, the number of exposures failing due to this error is down to 70 (all for two visits in 2013 for i-band). There are 157 total errors (excluding the 176 {{TaskError}} from DM-26587), so this remains as the single largest failure mode. It could be mitigated with more careful refinement of the value chosen for the source detection threshold, which is set with {{config.charImage.detection.thresholdValue}}. For my processing I chose the values of 10000 for i-band and 2500 for g-band based on the background level and variance for a few good visits I inspected, but a lower threshold for i-band would likely have allowed the two visits with errors to complete processing." [SEP] "Given that only two visits are failing with this error, I believe I have sufficient data to complete DM-25694 without fully resolving these failures."

summary: "Investigate IndexError when running ProcessCcd on Decam galactic bulge images"
description: """As reported in DM-25694, 6145 exposures (out of 20,679) failed with fatal errors during single frame processing of DECam galactic bulge data (in i- and g-band). Of these, 3108 were IndexErrors, e.g.    I need to identify the cause, or causes, of this error and suggest a solution. Implementation of the solution is expected to be made on a new ticket."""
comments: "Inspection of 14 different exposures that failed single frame processing with {{IndexError}} reveals that in every case the failure was due to too many PSF candidates being rejected due to the reduced chi^2 value for the candidate. The default chi^2 threshold is 2, but in at least one case the minimum chi^2 of any psf candidate was 201. By setting {{config.charImage.measurePsf.psfDeterminer['pca'].reducedChi2ForPsfCandidates=300.0}} I find that 12 of those 14 exposures now complete single frame processing without any errors. For the remaining two, one fails due to a {{LengthError}} (see DM-26586), and the remaining exposures fails with {{RuntimeError: No objects passed our cuts for consideration as psf stars}} (see DM-26588)." [SEP] "After increasing the chi-squared cut to {{config.charImage.measurePsf.psfDeterminer['pca'].reducedChi2ForPsfCandidates=300.0}}, the number of exposures that failed due to {{IndexError}} dropped from 3,108 to 1,054. " [SEP] "With 12 test exposures that failed even with {{config

"""It has become clear that, as discussed in Astier, et.al., we need to correct the non-linearity of the ASPIC amplifiers.  I created a linearizer with the current cubic polynomial fit using measurePhotonTransferCurve.py, then applied it using the ISR and re-ran measurePhotonTransferCurve.py.  It did not correct the """"bump"""" around 50,000 ADU, as shown in the attached NonLinearity_R22S11_C04_25Aug20.png.  Astier, et.al. recommend using a spline fit to better capture the nature of the bump.  I implemented this in a notebook at NCSA at:    and also uploaded it to git hub at:    It does a much better job at correcting the bump, although not as good as in Pierre's paper. You can see the improved correction in the attached NonLinearity_Correction_R22S11_C04_02Sep20.png.  Both of these plots are analogous to Figure 3 in Astier, et.al.      Note that the expTime data in this notebook was generated by applying the MONDIODE correction.  In order to do this, I modified ptc.py with the following lines.  While I added """"fudge factors"""" for three of the neutral density filters, I think this is not that critical since I ended up not using the low flux points in the spline fit.  Please contact me if any of this is not clear.  I think the next step is to implement the spline correction, which I think Chris Waters may already be working on.  Then we can try correcting the flux in the ISR using the spline fit and re-do the PTC curves.    """

summary: "Make the endpoint manager ignore not fully transferred rsync logs "
description: """In case of {{rsync}} base transfers, the list of the files that are ready to be ingested is inferred by parsing {{rync}}'s logs. Writing this log files is not an atomic operation so there is a good chance that manager's search plugin may start parsing a log file which has not been fully transferred yet. The file will be erroneously flagged as already parsed and any files added to that after that will be effectively ignored.    The goal is two-fold. Firstly, implement some heuristic which will allow the search plugin to determine if a log file has been fully transferred. Secondly, add a safeguard which will detect and log occurrences of such events."""
comments: "Changes approved for merging."

summary: "Make the endpoint manager handle empty files better"
description: """Empty files are not handled optimally by the endpoint manager. Firstly, all empty files, except the one which was discovered as first are treated as duplicates and deleted.  Secondly, the manager still tries to ingest the empty file to the database system which results in rather obscure errors coming from the ingest software being recorded in the database. The goal is to make the manager properly save all empty files in the storage area for future reference and provide a clear message in such a case."""

description: """Lander ([https://github.com/lsst-sqre/lander)] is currently hardcoded to produce landing pages with the old-style LSST branding. As part of updating our PDF landing pages to use Rubin Observatory branding (DM-25660) we want to generalize Lander with a plugin system so that the branding of the landing pages can be tweaked independently of Lander itself. This lets us ship separate documentation templates for different organizations (e.g. for Rubin operations vs Rubin construction).    The plugins should offer two categories of customization:   * Hooks to parse different types of LaTeX documents (i.e., Lander currently works with the lsstdoc class, but we could imagine also wanting to support aastex documents)   * Customized Jinja templates for HTML, along with customized CSS, JavaScript, and other assets."""

description: """Test Python asyncio socket behavior when one end of a connection hangs up and update https://confluence.lsstcorp.org/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=140284619 accordingly."""
comments: "Here are two command-line tasks that demonstrate some asyncio socket failure modes:  * socket_reader.py runs a TCP/IP socket server  * socket_writer.py runs a TCP/IP socket client    Normal operation consists of socket_writer writing several messages, the last of which is ""close"" that causes the reader to close its socket writer. This results in a normal shutdown:      To demonstrate one end or the other going away without cleaning up, specify a non-zero value for {{quit}}. On macOS I observe that the reader in each end can tell immediately when the other end goes away. There are failure modes that this does not test, such an ethernet cable being disconnected."

summary: "Jenkins stack-os-matrix fails due to leftover EUPS .lockDir"
description: """stack-os-matrix jobs occasionally fail with {{eups declare: Unable to take exclusive lock on /j/ws/stack-os-matrix/[...]: locks are held by [user=jswarm, pid=...]}}    The actual lock directory in the stack looks like {{/project/jenkins/prod/agent-ldfc-ws-1/ws/stack-os-matrix/adacff179f/lsstsw/stack/cb4e2dc/.lockDir}}.  Removing that and its contents resolves the problem but is manual (although a Jenkins pipeline, {{sqre/infra/clean-locks}} has been created to let anyone do this cleanup without requiring administrator intervention).    The problem appears to be correlated with manual termination of previous jobs.  The code in eups is supposed to clean up lock files and directories when interrupted, but perhaps something else is going on here.    Either fix the manual job termination to clean up properly, or turn off locking altogether for these jobs, if that is safe and feasible."""

"""In DM-26476 we discuss a need for an observation reason to be added to gen3 registry. This requires that we add the concept to ObservationInfo. This will be a string that can have any value. Then provide obs_lsst implementation that looks for TESTTYPE header and overrides it with that value. We do not yet know the name of the header that will be used for LSSTCam and ComCam.    Adding reason suggests that using """"science"""" for observation_type might be too generic since that was previously trying to serve both roles. Code already relies on """"science"""" for observation type though so it will be difficult to switch those to using reason instead."""

summary: "Ingest WISE PSD and MEP catalogs into ""small"" Qserv cluster at NCSA"
description: """Ingest two partitioned tables {{allwise_p3as_psd}} and {{allwise_p3as_mep}} of catalog {{WISE}} into the cluster using the new Ingest system. This effort is similar to the one of [DM-26390]. Note that input data of the catalog need to be repartitioned to the more efficient scheme:  * {{stripes}}: *340*  * {{sub-stripes}}: *3*  * {{overlap}}: *0.01667*    Put scripts and configurations used during the ingest into the dedicated Git package: https://github.com/lsst-dm/qserv-ncsa-wise"""
comments: "h1. Stats  The input data set:  * uncompressed size: *17 GB*  * files: *792*    The workflow equally divided *792* files into *132* files per each Qserv worker (*6* workers) and *12* processing _streams_ per worker. There were *72* such streams to be run in parallel across all workers. To optimize further operations with the input data, the workflow merged *11* files into a single {{TSV/CSV}} file to be processed by each stream. Thus the total number of input files was reduces to *72*.    The amount of data to be processed at each worker is reported below:  |2.3T|lsst-qserv-db31|  |2.3T|lsst-qserv-db32|  |2.3T|lsst-qserv-db33|  |2.3T|lsst-qserv-db34|  |3.1T|lsst-qserv-db35|  |4.1T|lsst-qserv-db36|    There were *72* super-transactions started to ingest catalog data (one such transaction per each loading stream). In particular we propose adopting Kafka in SQuaSH which replaces some of the actual components of the SQuaSH API (Redis, Celery) by EFD components (Kafka broker, zookeeper, InfluxDB Sink, S3 Sink and JDBC Sink connectors). summary: "Improved batch mode of ingesting table contributions into Qserv"
description: """The current implementation of the Ingest system has a binary tool *{{qserv-replica-file-ingest}}* providing the _batch_ mode for loading a list of contributions (read from input files) into various tables:    Where, the mandatory parameter *{{<contributions>}}* requires a *{{JSON}}*-formatted text file with an array of file contributions into potentially any tables using multiple transactions. Here is an example of such file:  {code:yaml}  [{""""worker-host"""":""""qserv-db01"""",""""worker-port"""":25002,""""transaction-id"""":123,""""table"""":""""Object"""",""""type"""":""""P"""",""""path"""":""""input/chunk_187107_overlap.txt""""},   {""""worker-host"""":""""qserv-db01"""",""""worker-port"""":25002,""""transaction-id"""":123,""""table"""":""""Object"""",""""type"""":""""P"""",""""path"""":""""input/chunk_187107.txt""""},   {""""worker-host"""":""""qserv-db02"""",""""worker-port"""":25002,""""transaction-id"""":123,""""table"""":""""Object"""",""""type"""":""""P"""",""""path"""":""""input/chunk_187108_overlap.txt""""},   {""""worker-host"""":""""qserv-db02"""",""""worker-port"""":25002,""""transaction-id"""":123,""""table"""":""""Object"""",""""type"""":""""P"""",""""path"""":""""input/chunk_187108.txt""""},   {""""worker-host"""":""""qserv-db01"""",""""worker-port"""":25002,""""transaction-id"""":123,""""table"""":""""Object"""",""""type"""":""""P"""",""""path"""":""""input/chunk_187109_overlap.txt""""},   {""""worker-host"""":""""qserv-db01"""",""""worker-port"""":25002,""""transaction-id"""":123,""""table"""":""""Object"""",""""type"""":""""P"""",""""path"""":""""input/chunk_187109.txt""""},   {""""worker-host"""":""""qserv-db02"""",""""worker-port"""":25002,""""transaction-id"""":123,""""table"""":""""Object"""",""""type"""":""""P"""",""""path"""":""""input/chunk_187110_overlap.txt""""},   {""""worker-host"""":""""qserv-db02"""",""""worker-port"""":25002,""""transaction-id"""":123,""""table"""":""""Object"""",""""type"""":""""P"""",""""path"""":""""input/chunk_187110.txt""""}  ]    qserv-replica-file-ingest FILE-LIST-TRANS <transaction-id> <table> <type> <contributions> [--auth-key=<key>] ...    This file would produce the same yield as the existing one if used like illustrated below:  {code:bash}  qserv-replica-file-ingest FILE-LIST-TRANS 123 Object P <contributions>  {code}    Advantages of the new batch mode:  * the file of contributions is reusable across transactions  * a scope of failures during ingest is limited to a single transactions    (!) Destinations where the tables contributions are meant to be ingested do not change after aborting and restarting transactions.  """
comments: "[~<USER> PR: https://github.com/lsst/qserv/pull/568" [SEP] "It looks good to me."

description: """Currently the data generator needs to be manually supplied with the number of Objects to create. This should instead be automatically computed from a density model defined in the specification file."""
comments: "Hi [~<USER>, here is a refactoring of DataGenerator and the column generators to enable spatial density models. The changes I made to do this are   # DataGenerator now holds the chunker and a spatial model object, instead of the columns each holding a chunker instance.   # The column generators now receive a SimpleBox instead of a chunk id, putting RaDecGenerator on par with the other columns and no longer needing to be special-cased,    # DataGenerator iterates over each box in the chunk, then over each column inside that box. I think that makes it easier to ensure that data columns are identical in edge-only mode.   # ForcedSourceGenerator now takes the box's Object table and the entire CcdVisit table, computes which visits overlap the chunk center, then generates all the FS fluxes based only on a single band magnitude from Object + the Gaussian random scatter. This is less realistic, but it eliminates the python loop and should be a huge speed-up.    I think the next two things that this feeds into are

summary: "Update LTD Conveyor to use GitHub Actions and tox"
description: """The purpose of this ticket is to update LTD Conveyor with many of SQuaRE current best practices to make it easier to maintain the LSST the Docs command line. Namely, this includes GitHub Actions, tox, pre-commit based linting, and possibly mypy as well."""
comments: "Released as version 0.7.0:         This release focuses on infrastructure improvements, and should not have a significant impact on users.    This tool is officially tested and supported on Python 3.7 and 3.8.  Python 3.6 is no longer officially supported.    Removed pins on third-party dependencies, including boto3, requests, uritemplate, and click.  In doing so, LTD Conveyor is easier to integrate into your existing Python environment.    We have migrated from Travis CI to GitHub Actions for testing, linting, documentation builds and deployment, and deployments to PyPI.  In addition to on-demand testing, GitHub Actions tests the package on a daily basis to ensure compatibility with third-party dependencies.    Internally, we use tox for running tests, linters, and to build documentation.  tox makes it possible for local test runs to use the same set up as CI.    The codebase is now automatically formatted with black and isort.  By using pre-commit, we ensure that formatting is always applied.    The codeb

description: """Attempting to run {{RawIngestTask}} with multiple processes on DM-25806, after fixing a pickling problem with {{Gen3DatasetIngestTask}}, produces the following stack trace:      Discussion on {{#dm-middleware}} revealed that this functionality has never been tested. Investigate this bug in a pure-{{obs_base}} context and fix the problem. Unit tests for pickling support of {{RawIngestTask}} and related classes are a good starting point."""
comments: "Note that the above stack trace was generated with {{processes=2}}, so I'm not sure why there are 5 workers." [SEP] "I've run into an implementation problem. {{Task}} serializes all its state (currently config, name, parent, and log) as a positional tuple. {{RawIngestTask}} requires that all of these (except config) be passed by keyword to the constructor, but the information about these arguments' identities is contained in {{Task}}. I can't think of an implementation of either {{\_\_reduce\_\_}} or {{\_\_setstate\_\_}} that can delegate to {{Task}}, but I'm loath to have a subclass depend on exactly what extra state {{Task}} defines.    [~<USER> or [~<USER>, since you've been pushing for heavier use of keywords for a while, how do you usually resolve these kinds of API mismatches?" [SEP] "Pickle fundamentally confuses me in its opaqueness. {{\_\_getnewargs_ex\_\_}} seems to be the only method that explicitly deals with keyword arguments." [SEP] "My preference is to use {{\_\_reduce\_\_}}, but with a privat

summary: "Inconsistency between the IDL and Control in LabVIEW"
description: """Inconsistency between the IDL and control in LabVIEW. The IDL file is generated by ts_sal v5.0.0. The following structure is from the IDL:  {code:c++}struct command_disable {   long TestID;   string<8> private_revCode; //private   double private_sndStamp; //private   double private_rcvStamp; //private   long private_seqNum; //private   string private_identity; //private   long private_origin; //private   long private_host; //private   boolean value;   };   #pragma keylist command_disable TestID{code}  The LabVIEW control is:   !disableCtrl.png|width=270,height=315!    It is clear that they are different.    In addition, if some attribute (such as *TestID*) should be given the value in the LabVIEW SAL monitor, the word of """"//private"""" should appear in the IDL file."""
comments: "I checked the IDL from *ts_SAL* v4.0.0, the *TestID* looks like something that is not belong to the structure:  {code:c++}struct logevent_summaryState {    long summaryState;       long priority;  };  #pragma keylist logevent_summaryState TestID  {code}  Therefore, maybe need to clarify what the *TestID* is.    " [SEP] "The LabVIEW control and IDL match now. Great!"

"""At present supporting simulation modes in CSCs requires overriding 3 methods: BaseCsc.implement_simulation_mode, BaseCsc.add_arguments, and BaseCsc.add_kwargs_from_args. Simplify this as follows:    Add a valid_simulation_modes argument to BaseCsc. Default it to None for backwards compatibility. If not None then do the following:  * Test the {{simulation_mode}} argument in the {{BaseCsc}} constructor.  * Make {{BaseCsc.handle_simulation_mode}} a no-op.  * Add a {{--simulate}} argument to {{BaseCsc.amain}}'s argument parser, if there is more than one valid simulation mode.    This will greatly reduce the amount of extra work needed by CSC authors to add a simulation mode.    Also convert at least one other package to use this new technique, to check the design."""
comments: "The ts_ATDome pull request demonstrates that the new technique does reduce boilerplate for supporting simulation. It is almost entirely deletion. The down side is that CSCs that do not support simulation will have to add one line to avoid a new deprecation warning.    I also cleaned up some CSC shutdown code (make sure the start task is canceled when closing the CSC), after running into a weird issue while writing the new tests.    Pull requests:  * https://github.com/lsst-ts/ts_salobj/pull/139  * https://github.com/lsst-ts/ts_ATDome/pull/32" [SEP] "Reviewed in GitHub." [SEP] "Merged to develop.    I also filed DM-26531 to track packages using this support (and any others that need v6, but this change is likely to be the dominant factor)."

description: """Raw files are being transferred to NCSA and ingested into LSST Butler repositories.  These services are now at the point where they need to be actively monitored.  A web page is currently being created by an external process checking status every so often.  We'd like there to be pushes of status to a monitoring system that can also send extra notifications (like an email or message to a particular slack channel) for particular errors (without spamming when lots of files fail ingestion).    Goals:    - Be able to tell at a glance if things are working/failing (where an absence may mean failures)    - Send out notifications to appropriate staff upon failures (no spamming when multiple failures)    Stretch Goal:    - Ability to group failures.    Data Backbone Buffer Manager technote with 10,000 foot view: https://dmtn-154.lsst.io/  Code: https://github.com/lsst-dm/dbb_buffmngrs_endpoint  [~<USER> and [~<USER> can provide input on what information/monitoring would be helpful.  [~<USER> is the current person responsible for the buffer managers code.    Subtasks are listed here.  Feel free to make them JIRA subtasks of this ticket.  subtask 1:     Determine whether there is an existing monitoring service (especially one already     being used by LSST at NCSA, e.g., for admins) that can be used.    subtask 2:  Gather suggestions/requirements for the monitoring from @<USER>and @ericm.  Make suggestions based upon knowledge of the to-be underlying monitoring service.  Note: the purpose of this ticket isn't to completely revamp what information is stored in the endpoint database or produce everything anyone might ever want.  Pick the ones that we can't live without to do in this initial ticket.  Additional monitoring can be added later.    subtask 3:  Run design from subtasks 1 and 2 by [~<USER> and [~<USER> (plus [~<USER>, [~<USER> and [~<USER> if available).  Does not need to be a real presentation or formal documentation.  Do not spend a lot of time formatting text, making pretty diagrams, etc.  Especially point out how one could tell at a glance if things are working/failing and how alerts would be sent out.    subtask 4:  Set up a development environment with current version and test with a couple files.  Then will have this ready for testing with new code changes.    subtask 5:  Implement monitoring changes to dbb_buffmgrs_endpoint.  Problems with monitoring should not halt/hang/delay other dbb_buffmgrs_endpoint duties.  (new ticket branch, push changes, pull request for [~<USER> to review (ticket in Review status), when review approved, merge changes to master)    subtask 6:  Deploy on NCSA Teststand endpoints. https://confluence.lsstcorp.org/display/~<USER>Internal+LSST+Data+Transfer+Notes  Announce taking service down to update on #ncsa-integ-teststand and #ncsa-transfers (and back up).  Can also keep an eye on those channels near this time to make sure you're not taking down during a special run of test data.  Update the deployment wiki page if needed.    subtask 7:  Add the monitoring to the 10,000 foot view technote (https://dmtn-154.lsst.io)."""

summary: "Allow CSCs to transition to ENABLED state on startup"
description: """The capability to send a CSC to ENABLED via appropriate command-line flags should be implemented. The CSC needs to do the following:    1. Accept a flag to indicate ENABLED as the desired top state (may already exist)  2. Accepts a flag for settingsToApply if necessary  3. CSC should transition through all states from STANDBY to ENABLED in order to maintain correct summary state transition history."""
comments: "This is a great suggestion. It should also simplify unit tests a bit, in that all CSCs can be commanded to start in ""ENABLED"" state even if they need configuration.    I am worried about how to handle the Hexapod and Rotator CSC (the summary state is kept in the low-level controller and the CSC may not have control over it). summary: "Add vectorized pure-python interface to convert ra/dec to and from x/y for SkyWcs"
description: """Currently, there is no direct way to convert numpy arrays of ra/dec to and from x/y with a {{SkyWcs}}, which leads to inefficiencies when computing {{SpherePoint}} for large numbers of positions.  Some of this would be fixed with DM-19235 which I believe is tricky because of the memory ordering.    As it turns out, AST supports using numpy arrays without any memory finagling, for example in {{suprême}}: https://github.com/LSSTDESC/supreme/blob/75a2008d203e28b3d42738374823f1f5e42d9db1/supreme/utils.py#L157    This ticket will add {{wcs.xyToRaDec}} and {{wcs.raDecToXY}} (or some suitable name) to {{wcsContinued.py}} to allow general access to these very fast ways of interfacing with the AST code.  I don't believe the names {{skyToPixel}} and {{pixelToSky}} can be used because pure-python methods can't do the overloading (plus these don't return or take quite the same type of data structures)."""

"""Sky objects are pseudo-objects placed within coadded imaging after object detection has taken place. They are randomly placed such that their footprint (currently an 8 pixel radius) does not overlap with any other detected source footprint. As such, these objects are very useful for QA purposes, e.g., sky level tracking.    This ticket adds two simple sky object performance metric tracters: the sigma-clipped mean sky object circular aperture flux value for all sky objects within a given tract; and the standard deviation of this distribution. These values are the same as those generated for the {{pipe_analysis}} skyObjects figure (e.g., [plot-t9615-HSC-I-skyObjects-hist.png|https://lsst.ncsa.illinois.edu/~<USER>w_2020_30_qaPlots/HSC-I/tract-9615/plot-t9615-HSC-I-skyObjects-hist.png]). Once generated, these metrics will ultimately be tracked over time by the [SQuaSH metrics tracking Chronograf|https://chronograf-demo.lsst.codes/sources/2/dashboards] (see [SQR-019|https://sqr-019.lsst.io/])."""

summary: "The low-level Rotator controller should use TAI time when processing track commands"
description: """All axis controllers are expected to use TAI date to understand and process tracking commands, which contain a position, a velocity, and a time at which the position applies. Tracking commands specify a path of constant velocity:    Where position0, velocity0 and tai0 are the values in the track command.    The axis controller is expected to interpolate between adjacent tracking commands, so that the commanded path is be more complicated than the equation shown above. But to do that interpolation correctly, it is necessary for the axis controller to know the current TAI date.    Unfortunately it appears that the rotator does not know anything about TAI time. It should read time from the CLOCK_TAI system clock for processing track commands.    It would also be helpful if it used CLOCK_TAI for other times, such as the timestamp in the telemetry header. The standard unix real time clock (which is what the rotator is using) is not well defined near a leap second, whereas CLOCK_TAI is completely uniform and unaffected by leap seconds."""
comments: "[~<USER> Please help to clarify what should be the equation you want to use to consider the CLOCK_TAI. The equation now just considers the time difference (tai - tai0)." [SEP] "When the pointing component send tracking commands to an axis controller, it sends a series of position, velocity and tai times. Let's call those posN, velN, taiN, where N is an index.  Each such command describes a unique path of constant velocity: {{pos(tai) = posN + velN dt, where dt = tai - taiN}}.  Furthermore taiN is always sent somewhat in advance. In other words when the axis controller receives the command taiN > current TAI.    Each axis controller is expected to interpolate between pairs of these tracking commands (which is why each is sent in advance) to come up with its own path that smoothly connects the tracking commands. I do not know if we told the vendors how to perform that interpolation (and if we did, whether Moog followed our specification). However, the simplest way to do the interpolation between two tracking

summary: "ScriptQueueCommander: support setting logLevel, etc. in the add command"
description: """Enhance the ScriptQueue commander to allow the user to specify logLevel and other options for the add command. Preferably make these optional arguments that use keywords, e.g. logLevel=40."""
comments: "The queue commander already supported specifying config on the command line -- either from a file or as a set of key=value pairs. So that meant adding key=value to support optional parameters wasn't going to work. What I chose to do is add -key=value for the optional fields, with sensible options for each field. For example ""-logLevel=40 -location=0"". I did not try to get parse enum names, but the help does print the allowed values for location and the usual values for logLevel.    Also I added a new log level option for starting the script queue commander, which sets the default log level for added scripts.    It is enough code that I am a bit worried about the lack of unit tests. But I have not yet figured out how to unit test these controllers.    Pull request: https://github.com/lsst-ts/ts_scriptqueue/pull/52" [SEP] "Reviewed on github."

summary: "Make BaseScript shut down gracefully for SIGTERM"
description: """OpenSplice is supposed to handle SIGTERM gracefully, but tests with the SAL/Kafka producers (which are stopped using SIGTERM) suggest that it is not. Killing a SAL/Kafka producer seems to corrupt the DDS data management process when using shared memory mode.    Pending SAL scripts on the script queue are also terminated with SIGTERM if they are stopped with the stopScripts command. So it is prudent to update BaseScript to handle SiGTERM with a graceful shutdown.    That said, I am curious if [~<USER> or [~<USER> have found that it is problematic to stop pending scripts. If that works then this ticket is unnecessary.    If we need this change then:    * Try updating Domain to catch SIGTERM. That would handle graceful termination of all salobj-based code (though ts_salkafka would still benefit from some special handling, to make shutdown quieter).  * Consider filing an ADLink case. Though making a simple """"how to reproduce"""" may be too difficult."""

summary: "Improve DateSystem enum docs to clarify EPOCH"
description: """I was not able to find documentation about the EPOCH value of {{daf.base.DateTime.DateSystem}}. From the C++ code, it looks like it means """"Julian Epoch Year"""", like the [astropy time format|https://docs.astropy.org/en/stable/api/astropy.time.TimeJulianEpoch.html#astropy.time.TimeJulianEpoch], but """"epoch"""" is also used elsewhere in the same code to refer to """"nanoseconds since the unix epoch"""", which is quite a different thing.    We need a short docstring on that enum (or elsewhere, if enum docstrings don't work) to make the values explicitly clear.    Also, the private `_getEpoch` could be similarly updated to specify that it is the Julian Epoch Year."""
comments: "I have added clarifications where I can.  I think the comment on the enum line should appear at least in the ""source code definition"" portion of the doxygen output.    If there's better terminology to use (""fractional year"" or something?), let me know." [SEP] "Two comments on the PR. Thanks for the quick fix." [SEP] "Changed to ""Julian epoch year"" in most places.  Jenkins wasn't run because this is purely a doc fix."

description: """When the client side JavaScript can no long detect a server, it pops up a dialog giving two options, one to dismiss, and one to start a server.  In the case of e.g. a VPN disconnection, neither of these suggestions are relevant.  I'd like to reword that dialog a bit to make it more generic to the reason it is happening and give advice that is helpful regardless of whether it is happening because the server exited or if it is still running but was not accessible for another reason.    The relevant block in the JupyterLab source starts [here|https://github.com/jupyterlab/jupyterlab/blob/4e7c446dccae5e82b1eba9af3eb1705917bc91a5/packages/hub-extension/src/index.ts#L152] and goes to line 162.    I think something like the following would be sufficient:      A savvy user may know that refreshing the browser window will solve the issue, but sending users to the hub home will let them see whether their server is, in fact, running and go to if if so.  If the server really has gone away they can start a new one from the hub home."""

summary: "Fix bug in insertFakes found during fakes RC processing."
description: """During the fakes RC processing [~<USER> found a bug in insertFakes which meant that not all of the sources were being added during processing. This came from some faulty logic introduced in a recent ticket to catch galaxies that had parameters outside of those that galsim can handle. Some of the stars also had their sersic indices set to numbers outside of this range, but this doesn't matter because they are going in as PSFs. However they were being removed before insertion. This resulted in very few stars making it into the images. This ticket is to correct that issue by making the logic check if either the sersic parameters are within the correct range or the object is a star."""

summary: "Investigate modest DIA Source count increase between w_15 and w_20"
description: """As noted in DM-25125, there was a notable uptick in the number of DIA Sources in reprocessing the HiTS2015 dataset with ap_pipe between April 2020 (circa w_2020_15) and May/June 2020 (w_2020_20 or later).    I originally suspected the increase was due to an ISR bug (fixed in DM-25149), but now I think it was due to a change in image differencing, because the calexp source counts did not change.    The new sources are prominently concentrated near CCD edges - did pixel masking change while the overall number of DIA Sources remained unchanged, perhaps? - but despite many new sources near edges, some do also appear smattered all across CCDs.    The most substantial difference imaging change in the relevant timeframe was the decorrelation algorithm (DM-24371). In the ap_verify CI metrics, we noted a decrease in the total number of unassociated DIA Objects as well as changes in the ratio of DIA Sources to direct image sources for each CCD. However, the templates used by ap_verify CI changed to higher resolution at the same time, so it is difficult to disentangle. (Whereas the templates have not changed in my ap_pipe reruns since January 2020.)    This ticket is to actually investigate the significant (~8%) increase in DIA Sources."""

summary: "In salobj work around the OpenSplice bug that causes read problems when a writer process shuts down"
description: """[~<USER> found a problem with licensed OpenSplice 6.10.4 and a version of licensed 6.10.3 as well: when a writer process goes away the salobj reader may be come unresponsive. This especially affects the script queue, since script processes are constantly shutting down. I filed this as case 00020647.    [~<USER> also found a workaround: use  a read mask of dds.DDSStateKind.ANY_INSTANCE_STATE and ignore invalid samples in the Python code. This slows things down a bit, but seems to work with OpenSplice 6.10.4. Update the code accordingly.    We will use that fix until we are sure it's safe to do otherwise.    Add this to prerelease 6 and also release a new version of 5.17 with this fix."""

summary: "Change how dimensions are stored in a Butler repository"
description: """Dimensions.yaml should be locked in and read-only as soon as the schema is created during the makeRepo phase.  At the moment there is nothing stopping a repository from breaking if dimensions.yaml is updated in daf_butler and there is nothing stopping a user editing the config file after repository creation and breaking things.    We propose to remove dimensions.yaml from ButlerConfig and have it as an explicit argument for makeRepo (defaulting to {{pkg://lsst.daf.butler/configs/dimensions.yaml}}) which will then ensure that the dimensions configuration is locked in for that repository.    We will still require that it be possible to migrate a registry to a new dimensions definition but that migration should be by explicit request."""
comments: "I have prototyped some of this on DM-26336 (the part about how to serialize the DimensionUniverse to database tables), and it seems quite doable.  That got me thinking that we might also want to make DimensionUniverse mutable, and have Registry APIs for adding new dimensions and columns dynamically to a repo (analogous to DatasetType registration); the config file in daf_butler would then just represent the initial/default.  I'm writing a Confluence page to document what that prototyping ticket came up with, and will have more details there." [SEP] "Now that I've gotten my head out of CALIBRATION collections, I'm going to try to write down the thoughts I had on this topic while prototyping, in the hopes that'll help [~<USER> and I coordinate as we both make rather big changes to the dimensions system (mine will be over on DM-26692, or subtask tickets thereof).  My thoughts are not very well organized, and I'd be happy to have a live chat of some kind about them, but hopefully just writing them down now will

description: """Science Pipelines-based containers are very large.  See if they can be shrunk."""
comments: "The compressed size of the containers is down quite a bit, from ~2.4GB 1 year ago to ~1.5GB today. That can be improved a bit further with ""conda clean -f""    A non-trivial amount of conda-installed software size is just qt (via pyqt, for matplotlib), boost, gcc, git, and git-lfs. These account for around 1.7GB of uncompressed space. on disk of the conda installation.    The uncompressed ""stack"" directory is 1.4GB, in lsstsqre/centos:d_latest (As of today)    So uncompressed stack with the deps above is about 3.5GB.    A ""du -hs /"" on d_latest is 4.4GB, after a ""conda clean -f"". So 3.5 of those 4.4GB (80%) so about 900MB for the rest of the remainder of conda dependencies.    Removing Qt might be possible - it's not needed for Mac's matplotlib, but I haven't investigated this. Removing compilers is definitely possible, but not sure if it's desirable."

description: """When a second tab to nublado is opened before the connected one is closed, JupyterLab (I think, but it could be hub) assumes you want a new view into the same container.  In a situation where a user is sharing views, this seems like a plausible feature, but in our situation that's not possible.    Workspaces are somewhat confusing to users since it happens transparently.  Adding to this, a feature people expect from JupyterLab is to present them a view that is the same as the last time they were there, but if there is a cached workspace that is picked up, it can show a view that is very different from the one they just saw, which is probably more confusing than simply being blank.    It seems prudent to turn this feature off, if possible, unless there is a fundamental reason we want to keep it."""

comments: "Similarly to DM-26046, I re-made the slides with summary plots to compare fits within the standard bounding box and the expanded one (20 pix on each side). The expanded fit regions mask out other footprints, i.e. it's only adding more (putative) sky pixels. Slides 4,5,7,9,11,13 are the only ones that are different from the originals. What I learned:   * Photometric accuracy does not improve with larger fitting regions; there are at best very marginal reductions in the 1-sigma scatter for bright galaxies but these are easily offset by the significantly larger 2-sigma scatter for faint galaxies.   * The runaway size growth problem is much worse (slides 11 & 13), but it is kind of similar to meas_modelfit in that the outliers are so much larger than 'normal' galaxies that they're very distinct and can be selected/separated.   * Nonlinear deblending helps to mitigate the runaway size problem even more so than for non-expanded fit regions... but only relatively speaking - the problem remains much worse. description: """Run CTI correction code by A. As far as calibration products, right now I use a custom class for easy read/write to a FITs table, but for each amplifier I use: 2x parameters for the electronics correction and a look-up table for the trap (stored as pickle file of the classes I used to define the traps). For running the code at NCSA: """"correct_images.py"""" could be run on BOT data at NCSA as long as """"ctisim"""" is in the path, plus config parameters and information about traps. summary: "Prototype and design work for dimensions/queries system improvements"
description: """daf.butler.registry.queries is getting creaky and hard to work with; usage patterns and needs have slowly diverged from what it was originally designed to do, and it's accumulated a lot of bad encapsulation, tight coupling, weird and hard-to-document method preconditions.  This has gotten bad enough that it's very hard to include support for querying CALIBRATION collections on DM-24432 without major changes.    We also have a number of related changes on the horizon that we should at least consider in any cleanup or refactoring we do now:  * We need to make additive changes to the dimensions system (adding new dimensions, adding new metadata columns) less disruptive in terms of schema versioning.  * We have discussed replacing the commonSkyPix/HTM-based spatial joins with a system where the user explicitly declares up front the combinations of skymap, instrument, and skypix systems they want to use together, and then we just instantiate those relationship tables up-front.  That should make for faster queries, simpler query code (no trimming of query rows in postprocessing) and perhaps more configurable flexibility in the future for the kinds of relationships that can exist.  * We need to be able to give users more control over which variants of at least some spatial relationships are used in a query.  The use case we have is """"give me all visit+detector data IDs for which the visit overlaps the tract, even if the visit+detector doesn't, and all HTM IDs at some level that overlap that visit+detector even if they don't overlap the tract"""", but I'd like to frame that problem in a more abstract sense before trying to solve it.      This ticket is only for design/prototype work, not actually implementing all of the above changes on master."""
comments: "Most of the prototyping work done here is captured in https://confluence.lsstcorp.org/display/DM/Conclusions+and+Proposals+from+DM-26336+Prototyping, which is a high-level summary of changes I'd like to make in the future.  I think that's worth reviewing, even though there probably isn't enough there for anyone else to take those descriptions and do the work, if they want to take advantage of the prototyping I've done.    The full prototyping branch remains at u/<USER>DM-26336/prototyping, but it's really best considered just ""notes to self"" at this point.  I can clean up and describe various bits of it more as needed in order for others to take over some of the work (e.g. DM-26407).  But I don't think that's worth doing across the board given that I'll probably be doing a lot of it.    There is also a tickets/DM-26336 branch with a PR (https://github.com/lsst/daf_butler/pull/368) that I'd like to get reviewed and merged now.  It includes some non-disruptive, noncontroversial baby steps towards the prototy

summary: "Remove obs dependency from ap_verify_testdata and ap_pipe_testdata"
description: """The {{ap_verify}} dataset framework was originally designed on the principle that {{ap_verify}} itself should not depend on any obs packages or on any datasets, and that users should set up only those that they need. Datasets have a dependency on the {{obs}} package they need, so that the package is (almost) guaranteed to be available if the dataset is set up. As far as I know, the current CI system assumes this behavior.    However, this causes a problem with {{ap_verify_testdata}}, because the package is included in some Stack builds despite being only an optional dependency of {{ap_verify}}. This causes frequent downloads of {{ap_verify_testdata}}, which would be prevented if {{ap_verify_testdata}} had no dependencies and it was {{ap_verify}} that guaranteed the obs package. This change would make {{ap_verify_testdata}} unusable for general processing, but this is already the case because {{ap_verify_testdata}} is not registered with {{ap_verify}} as a known dataset (but we would no longer be able to remove this special-casing in the future).    {{ap_pipe_testdata}} also has a dependency (on {{obs_decam}}), but is a conventional test data repository rather than an {{ap_verify}} dataset. Its dependencies should also be reworked as part of this ticket.    I think we can keep {{ap_pipe}} and {{ap_verify}} from having hard dependencies on obs packages if we make them optional. However, this requires reworking the test code that checks if testdata are available; see [jointcal|https://github.com/lsst/jointcal/blob/2ce9f257647894f085e5c18e5f0136ea5263cc74/tests/test_jointcal_decam.py#L49-L57] for one way to do this.    Removing the {{obs_lsst}} dependency will also break {{ap_verify_testdata}}'s Gen 3 maintenance script. However, as long as it's obvious to the script caller that the problem is a missing package, I don't think anything needs to be done."""

summary: "Mobu sometimes gets stuck waiting for lab to spawn"
description: """Sometimes when mobu is creating a lab, it gets stuck.  This happens because mobu has to keep polling the spawn URL until it gets a redirect (to the lab).  This is the only way to know when the hub is finished spawning.    So in mobu, we keep retrying until we get a redirect.  But we shouldn't retry forever!  We need a timeout of the number of retries, so we can make progress and try again (and alert on the expiration of the number of retries)"""

"""[Discussions on slack|https://lsstc.slack.com/archives/C2JPT1KB7/p1597173742073200] raised a very valid concern about our usage of connections that may be expected to stay alive for long time (at least the duration of a PipelineTask's runQuantum), noting that SQLAlchemy's connection pooling functionality (which we currently turn off) _might_ address this.    The solution is not as simple as having our {{Database}} class hold onto an {{Engine}} object and make a new {{Connection}} every time it is called, however - many {{Registry}} operations involve multiple calls to {{Database}}, and because these are expected to be part of the same transaction, they must all be executed by the same {{Connection}}.    One way to address this would be to split the {{Database}} interface into a pair of classes, one per-engine and one per-connection. We could probably make only the former an ABC, giving it protected virtual methods that take a {{Connection}} object as an argument. The per-connection object would be concrete and final, and just hold the connection state and public forwarders to those protected methods.    I'll have to think harder about how best to provide access to a per-connection object from the per-engine object, and which of those two should get the {{transaction}} method; I think that's the big open question.    Before we embark on this change, it might be good to try to verify (perhaps just by reading the docs) that using SQLAlchemy connection pooling actually _does_ provide some insulation from broken connections, as opposed to just seeming like something that _ought_ to provide some insulation. If we need to implement our own reconnection logic, I'd like to know that up front."""
comments: "Furthermore, it appears that when multiprocessing is used and therefore processes are forked, you need to be careful that SQLAlchemy usage before the fork is kept separate from SQLAlchemy usage after the fork -- in particular, connections and connection pools cannot be shared between the two."

summary: "Determine the compensation models for the Hexapod, including coefficients"
description: """Determine a suitable model for Hexapod compensation, with coefficients."""
comments: "Thanks to [~<USER> for the following data sources:    Compensation for elevation:  * M2 hexapod: https://github.com/<USER>hexrot/blob/master/LUT/M2%20Hexapod%20Motions%20in%20Elevation%20Axis%202020%2007%2023.xlsx  * Camera hexapod: https://github.com/<USER>hexrot/blob/master/LUT/Camera%20Hexapod%20Motions%20in%20Elevation%20Axis%202020%2007%2023.xlsx    Compensation for temperature:  * Document 7334 (a powerpoint presentation) page 9, which lists the fit coefficient of expansion.    I used the elevation spreadsheets and the attached code to fit various models for elevation. I found:  * A Fourier series worked best: f(el) = C0 + C1 sin(el) + C2 cos(el0 + C3 sin(2 el) + C4 cos(2 el) + .... This model can also be used for azimuth, since it wrap around at 0/360.  * A standard polynomial worked well: f(el) = C0 + C1 el + C2 el^2 + .... This is not suitable for azimuth compensation.  * A ""cosine polynomial"" did not work very well: f(el) = C0 + C1 cos(el) + C2 cos(el)^2 + .... This can be used for azimuth compen

summary: "Investigate situation of deleting and adding to the same butler collection simultaneously"
description: """In DM-25818 we changed datastore.put such that it overwrites existing files. This assumes that registry knows what is going on.    In the ticket the possibility was raised that a dataset could be trashed (which does not delete the file) and another file with the same dataId be put simultaneously. These would share the same filename but different dataset_id and results in the possibility of one process writing the  new file just before emptyTrash is called deleting what it thinks is the old file.    A suggestion was to include the dataset_id in the file name."""

summary: "Support the New Enum Syntax in IDL File"
description: """Support the new enum syntax in IDL file: (1) shared enum such as the *SummaryState*, and (2) assign the enum value in <Enumeration> of xml. Use the *Test* subsystem as an example, the structure of summary state looks like this:    {color:#4c9aff}struct logevent_summaryState \{{color}  {color:#4c9aff}    long summaryState;{color}  {color:#4c9aff}    long priority;{color}  {color:#4c9aff} };{color}    There is no information to say summaryState is a enum (e.g. *// enum :*).    In addition, the following part in *Test* xml does not reflect to IDL file:  {color:#4c9aff}<Enumeration>Int0ValueEnum_Zero=0, Int0ValueEnum_Two=2, Int0ValueEnum_Four=04, Int0ValueEnum_Five=0x05</Enumeration>{color}"""

description: """Working on the new pipeline_check package I realized that the 33MB dark and bias files can trivially be compressed using fpack to be 5MB each.    Compressing these single data array FITS files (GZIP_1 or GZIP_2) then breaks ExposureFitsReader with a SEGV when trying to read the mask that does not exist:        The compression confuses the reader because compression creates an extra hdu binary table. MaskedImageFitsReader does not look at the EXTTYPE header and so tries to read the next extension as if it's a mask.    I'm not entirely sure why we write FITS files with EXTTYPE headers but then don't use them when we read.    The most important issue here is the SEGV.    To reproduce run {{fpack -g2}} on the {{CALIB/DARK/2013-11-03/NONE/DARK-2013-11-03-010.fits}} from {{testdata_ci_hsc}}.  Then try to read the .fits.fz file with ExposureFitsReader:    {code:python}  from lsst.afw.image import ExposureFitsReader  reader = ExposureFitsReader(""""dark.fits.fz"""")  e = reader.read()  {code}    [~<USER> points out that we probably should be reading these as Image and not Exposure but that might require invasive changes to pipelines.    A more generic solution would be for MaskedImageFitsReader to query for EXTTYPE and if there are no MASK and VARIANCE extensions, skip trying to read them regardless of the number of extensions found.    """
comments: "I'm inclined to call this a blocker on the release of the pipelines_check package because 30MB of data files in git vs 100MB of data files is a big difference (the flats can be compressed since they have variance/mask and the raw is already compressed)." [SEP] "I've done a naive fix of skipping mask/variance reading if the mask/variance readers have no fits file associated with them (because nextHdu returned null). This fixes my problem without trying to get into looking at EXTTYPE headers (it looks like there is a check for EXTTYPE at one point).    Should I add a compressed dark to afwdata and test I can read it?" [SEP] "One significant comment about warnings and the needAllHdus parameter on the PR - should be easy to address once we decide what we _should_ do, and I'm hoping the usage context that brought this up will make that clear.    Adding a test file afwdata sounds like a good idea.  I seem to recall test data being added to afw instead more recently (all fairly small files), and now that I think ab

summary: "Remove references to data ID query language as ""SQL-like"""
description: """Several pages in the {{daf_butler}} documentation describe the data ID query expressions as """"SQL-like"""", including (but not limited to) the [Overview|https://pipelines.lsst.io/modules/lsst.daf.butler/dimensions.html#spatial-and-temporal-dimensions], the documentation for [Registry|https://pipelines.lsst.io/py-api/lsst.daf.butler.registry.Registry.html#lsst.daf.butler.registry.Registry.queryDatasets], and the [dimension expression documentation|https://pipelines.lsst.io/modules/lsst.daf.butler/queries.html#dimension-expressions].    However, the expression language is neither a subset (as advertised) nor a superset of SQL, and attempting to write queries on the assumption that they are SQL-like leads to user confusion:  * {{column BETWEEN 1 AND 10}} is wrong, it should be {{column IN (1..10)}}  * There is no way to [query for the absence of a value|https://lsstc.slack.com/archives/C2JPT1KB7/p1595614139193900]; in particular, no equivalent to SQL's {{NULL}} (or, for that matter, the {{IS}} operator)    I recommend that comparisons to SQL be removed from the documentation, and replaced with links to the query language page. This will encourage users to read that page instead of assuming they are already familiar with the language."""

summary: "Graph builder crashes on unregistered dataset types"
description: """When running the graph builder in a context where a run exists (as a side effect of previous Butler operations) but has never been populated, I get the following error:      In {{CmdLineFwk}}, dataset type registration is normally done after the graph is built, so I assume {{GraphBuilder}} is not supposed to assume dataset types are present?"""
comments: "{quote}  I assume GraphBuilder is not supposed to assume dataset types are present?  {quote}    Correct.  I believe this was introduced in a behavior change in daf_butler, where {{queryDatasets}} ""helpfully"" reports that the given dataset type has not been registered, so it's silly to be asking it to query for datasets of that type.  That's probably the right behavior for interactive use, because is indeed a better diagnostic and could help to catch logic bugs, but it needs to be caught and treated as a non-error in this context."

description: """It would be useful to have a dedicated API for being able to call {{CmdLineFwk}} (or some other activator) from Python code. My own application for this is {{ap_verify}}, which in Gen 3 combines some repository management with running a Pipeline on the repository it set up.    Desirable features:  * clear separation of concerns  * ability to edit the {{Pipeline}} object before committing to graph generation  * ability to delegate repository details (in particular, the collections to run on) to an existing {{Butler}} object  * uniform interface, allowing different activators to serve as drop-in replacements for each other"""
comments: "I'd like to cast a +1 for this as well.  The ability to invoke a pipeline from a service process is, I believe, going to become an important part of the image service architecture, particularly for things like diffim- and PVI-recreation-on-demand.  It would be useful for this not to have to invoke an external command-line executable in all cases.    This capability was part of the original SuperTask design, though it may have been deprioritized in the mean time."

summary: "lsstsw is slow on centos7/centos8 for conda transactions"
description: """Whenever bin/deploy is ran, it will attempt to reinstall a conda environment even if that exact conda environment is already deployed. On jenkins workers for linux, this is really slow because the transactions are very slow over networked file systems due to the large amount of small files.         Some kind of fast path could probably be created - particularly when we are using lock files. Simply verifying an existing environment has the same {{conda list --explicit}} output that the environment we are asking to install should be enough to bypass the extra step."""
comments: "From my understanding, especially in Jenkins, nothing is going to change the environment.     Therefore, we could just check if the environment exists, and avoid re-deploying in case it is.    Or we could add a flag to force: do not redeploy if it exists, so we keep the current behavior as default." [SEP] "As long as the env is specified by git hash, I agree that looking at the contents is overkill.  In other cases, checking the contents doesn't seem too expensive and is a bit safer." [SEP] "Considering that an environment reference may be a tag instead of a hash and that tags may change (this is a different problem in my opinion), it may make sense to check also that the content of the environment hasn't changed."

summary: "Investigate unusually large memory footprint in Qserv czar"
description: """When Qserv is processing many parallel _shared scan_ queries changes in the memory footprint of the {{mysql-proxy}} may indicate a possible memory leak in the application. Here and example of the snapshots taken by UNIX tool {{top}} *after* launching one batch of 80 shared scan queries at a time:    One more snapshot was taken while processing another batch of 80 queries at a level of 50% done (on average):    It looks like there is a big pile of memory allocated and held by the process. This memory is not released after finishing each batch (of 80 queries).    A goal of this ticket is to investigate a source of the memory consumption, understand why the memory is not released after completing the queries, and find a fix for the problem."""

summary: "Allow butler export to easily skip some dimensions"
description: """When using butler export to save datasets to YAML, we tend to include relevant instrument dimensions such as instrument, physical_filter, and detector.    In many cases where you want to import into an existing repository we have already run {{butler register-instrument}} and these dimensions have already been defined. Import therefore fails. Currently we have to edit the YAML file. It would be nice if it was possible for either these instrument dimensions to be skipped (optionally) on export or else have the dimension duplication be ignored on import.    It's possible that skipping on import would be more robust.    I believe that currently skipping named dimensions on export is quite involved.    """

summary: "Sort out where formatter and storage class definitions are located"
description: """At the moment we have a situation where every time someone adds a new storage class they have to change a couple of files in daf_butler. Firstly the storage class definition and secondly the list of formatters.    daf_butler is not a good place for these and we already have a mechanism for allowing butler configurations to be merged. We have already had a situation in gen2 where everyone has to do a little patch to obs_base for each new thing and there must be a better way.    I think we can add to DAF_BUTLER_CONFIG_PATHS in the EUPS table files in the obs packages, but we should consider whether we want every package that defines a class to also define its own storage classes entry (and default formatter) rather than having a class defined in meas_algorithms but registered in obs_base.  This might lead to a proliferation of paths to search and slow down butler instantiation.    What I would like to do is use package resources for accessing them like we do in daf_butler itself but I don't think EUPS table files are set up to handle that -- firstly it might not like {{resource://}} in the PATH-like environment variable and secondly if I had a DAF_BUTLER_CONFIG_RESOURCES environment variable the resource name of {{lsst.meas.algorithms/configs}} does not match PRODUCT_DIR so EUPS won't be able to unsetup."""

description: """With the hsc test data, there are non finite values in the image. The zogy score image calculation does not handle correctly the inf/nan propagation. Set the score image to 0 at these pixels instead of resetting the {{nan/inf}} that produces the warning when e.g. compared in {{less}} operation.               """

When I checked out the test run result, it turned out that the S=0 zero block is actually overwritten by detection (DM-26398)." [SEP] "Please see the pull request for a few requested changes." summary: "Ensure that filters are defined in pipetask multiprocessing"
description: """In DM-26119 I learned that when pipetask uses multiprocessing we never instantiate an {{Instrument}} and therefore we never define the filters for that instrument in the global singleton.    In single process mode it's fine because at some point an Instrument is created.    Modify pipetask multiprocessing such that the dataIds are scanned for the """"instrument"""" dimension and we call {{Instrument.fromName(dataId[""""instrument""""], registry)}}.  Currently we only expect one instrument.    When the singleton is removed it's likely that some related initialization will be needed to register the filters but we assume that would allow the same initialization for multiple instruments.    CC/ [~<USER>, [~<USER> in case they have come across this problem before."""
comments: "I want to add that it's not just in multiprocessing that the {{Instrument}} isn't instantiated.  I've noticed that when running my in-progress {{fgcmcal}} {{pipetask}} that I have to instantiate the {{Instrument}} manually.  So in a single process for some tasks, the {{Instrument}} is created somewhere, incidentally, but not through any explicit pattern.  Should this be explicitly done somewhere in {{pipetask}} as well/instead of the multiprocessing run?" [SEP] "[~<USER>, is it possible to reproduce that crash you saw on DM-26119? I probably don't want to write a unit test for that but I need a way to test that fix is going to work." [SEP] "Also what is that ""filter yaml"" that you mentioned there, where do I get it from?"
